methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $16M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Fuse Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Nuclear Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M+

data.narrative:
Curio Solutions is a Series A nuclear energy company founded by Ed McGinnis and Pete Zweifel that has raised $16M+. The valuation assessment draws from a broad nuclear energy peer universe of 11 companies (3 public, 8 private), including comparable-stage and later-stage players such as Fuse Energy, Oklo, TerraPower, X-Energy, and Kairos Power.

Stage-progression methodology is the primary applicable approach, using the $16M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A advancement. This method yields an implied valuation of $32M. A peer-median approach cannot be reliably applied; while 8 private nuclear energy peers exist, insufficient same-stage or narrow-tier comparables (fewer than 3 datapoints meeting tight filters) preclude a defensible median-based cross-check without risk of distortion.

The triangulated valuation range is $27M (low) to $37M (high), centered on $32M. This range reflects the single-method limitation and carries material uncertainty around capital efficiency, technical de-risking, and path to commercial deployment—standard for early-stage frontier technology. The analysis would benefit from additional same-stage nuclear energy comparables or direct cost-of-capital benchmarking as more peers mature into funding milestones.
